surface.name: Latest developer-platform Quickstart through local app-card preview
surface.selection_basis: HubSpot's Introduction directs new developers to Quickstart to create their first app, and the current Quickstart explicitly builds, deploys, installs, locates, and previews a boilerplate app card. This is the official latest-platform route, distinct from legacy private-app and public-app token paths.

alternatives_considered.name: Legacy private app access token
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: HubSpot positions it as a legacy route for limited API-token needs, while the current Quickstart is the named first-app path on the latest developer platform.

alternatives_considered.name: OAuth public or marketplace app
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It adds distribution, scopes, redirect URL, install consent, authorization-code exchange, access token, and refresh behavior.

alternatives_considered.name: Direct API request with hs api
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It requires selecting an endpoint and Personal Access Key permission, and does not exercise the current first-app Quickstart.

alternatives_considered.name: Full hs project create flow
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: HubSpot tells new app developers to use the streamlined hs get-started Quickstart first.

entry_point.developer_intent: Build, deploy, install, and preview HubSpot's boilerplate Get Started App Card on a contact record.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has no authenticated HubSpot CLI configuration or local quickstart project.
The developer has or can create a standard HubSpot account with permission to use developer tools.
Node.js 20 or later and npm can be installed.
The selected testing account contains at least one contact record to open for the final preview.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Successfully deploy the quickstart app and preview its Get Started App Card.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The boilerplate app is built, deployed, installed, assigned to the default contact record view, served from local development, and visibly rendered on a contact.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: A contact's Overview tab displays Get Started App Card with the Developing locally badge while hs project dev is running.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Preview your app card ends with the card visible on a contact record with a Developing locally badge; the following section is Continue local development and the final heading says the quickstart app was successfully deployed.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The visible app card proves the account, Personal Access Key, CLI, project generation, cloud build/deploy, app install, card placement, testing-account selection, and local development bridge all worked. Editing the example React component is the next development loop, not necessary for first visibility.

prerequisites.requirement: A standard HubSpot account; it may be free or paid.
A user whose permissions allow a Personal Access Key with Developer tools access to upload developer projects.
Node.js 20 or later, npm, a terminal, and a browser.
At least one contact in the selected testing account for the final app-card preview.
Access to npm, HubSpot's API and app, and the local development connection.

primary_path.action: Open HubSpot's current developer-platform Quickstart.
details: The guide promises a simple boilerplate app on the latest developer platform.
primary_path.input: Intent to build a first HubSpot app.
primary_path.output: Ordered account, CLI, project, install, location, and preview path.
primary_path.success_signal: Set up your local environment is visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: None documented.

primary_path.action: Create a HubSpot account if one is not already available.
details: The Quickstart links to account creation before its numbered work.
A standard account can be free or paid and provides the developer overview.
primary_path.success_signal: The user can sign in to HubSpot.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The linked account-creation flow and any email or identity verification are not enumerated in the developer docs.

primary_path.action: Install Node.js 20 or later if it is not already available.
details: HubSpot's CLI installation guide requires Node.js and supports versions 20 or later.
primary_path.success_signal: The supported Node and npm environment is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Operating-system installation is delegated to Node or package-manager guidance.

primary_path.action: Install the latest HubSpot CLI globally.
details: Run npm install -g @hubspot/cli.
The CLI guide identifies 8.6.0 as the latest recommended version at research time.
primary_path.input: Node.js, npm, registry access, and global install permission.
primary_path.success_signal: The installation completes and hs can run.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The CLI guide links to npm EACCES guidance if global installation lacks permission.

primary_path.action: Run hs account auth.
details: This connects HubSpot local development tools to the account.
primary_path.success_signal: The CLI opens or presents the Personal Access Key flow.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An existing key can be supplied with the command flag instead.

primary_path.action: Follow the prompt into HubSpot and generate or view a Personal Access Key.
details: The key can also be found under Development, Keys, Personal Access Key.
Key scopes are limited by the current user's HubSpot permissions.
Developer tools access is needed to upload projects.
primary_path.input: Authenticated HubSpot user with adequate permission.
primary_path.output: Personal Access Key passed back to or available for the CLI.
primary_path.success_signal: The CLI receives the key and authenticates the account.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Insufficient user permissions can produce a key that cannot upload developer projects.

primary_path.action: Choose whether to make this HubSpot account the CLI default.
details: The Quickstart recommends setting it as default so later commands do not need --account.
primary_path.output: Default-account choice stored in the global CLI config.
primary_path.success_signal: The CLI records the account, and if chosen, marks it default.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If not default, later commands need explicit account selection.

primary_path.action: Run hs get-started.
details: This initializes the streamlined boilerplate project.
primary_path.success_signal: The CLI asks what to build.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Authentication or CLI errors prevent initialization.

primary_path.action: Select App in the get-started prompts.
primary_path.success_signal: The CLI advances to project details.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Other project types lead outside this quickstart.

primary_path.action: Give the project a name.
details: The later cd command uses this local project name.
primary_path.success_signal: The CLI accepts the name.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The guide does not enumerate naming validation.

primary_path.action: Set the project's local directory.
details: The current working directory is the default.
primary_path.success_signal: The CLI accepts the directory.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A non-writable or conflicting path can block file creation.

primary_path.action: Choose to upload the generated project to HubSpot.
details: The upload initializes the first build and deploy.
primary_path.input: Generated local boilerplate and selected HubSpot account.
primary_path.success_signal: The CLI begins the HubSpot build.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Network, permission, account, or project validation errors can reject upload.

primary_path.action: Build and deploy the uploaded quickstart project.
details: HubSpot processes the first cloud build before installation can begin.
primary_path.success_signal: The CLI reports deployment and prompts to install the app.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: This is an external build wait. The Quickstart does not state duration or detailed build-error recovery.

primary_path.action: Press Enter to confirm installation in the account.
details: The command opens a browser to the app installation page.
primary_path.success_signal: HubSpot's install page opens.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Browser launch failure requires opening the relevant HubSpot page manually, but the quickstart does not specify the fallback URL.

primary_path.action: Review the expected unverified-app warning.
details: The warning appears because the new app has not been reviewed through HubSpot's marketplace verification process.
The Quickstart says it is safe to proceed for the app the developer just built.
primary_path.success_signal: The confirmation checkbox and Connect App are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: None documented.

primary_path.action: Select the checkbox confirming installation.
primary_path.success_signal: Connect App can be used.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Without confirmation, installation cannot proceed.

primary_path.action: Click Connect App.
primary_path.output: App installed in the HubSpot account.
primary_path.success_signal: The installation success page appears.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Installation or permission errors are not decomposed in the Quickstart.

primary_path.action: Confirm the installation success page.
primary_path.success_signal: The page offers Continue to manage App Card view.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the page does not appear, installation did not complete.

primary_path.action: Click Continue to manage App Card view.
details: HubSpot redirects to app-card settings.
primary_path.success_signal: Manage locations is visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: None documented.

primary_path.action: Click Manage locations.
primary_path.success_signal: Get Started App Card can be selected.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: None documented.

primary_path.action: Select the checkbox next to Get Started App Card.
details: This adds the card to the middle column of the default contact record view.
primary_path.success_signal: The checkbox is selected.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: None documented.

primary_path.action: Click Save.
details: This saves location changes and closes the sidebar.
primary_path.success_signal: The sidebar closes with the card location retained.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: None documented.

primary_path.action: Click Complete card setup in the Get Started App Card section.
details: This finishes HubSpot's card-setup walkthrough.
primary_path.success_signal: The setup walkthrough completes.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: None documented.

primary_path.action: Navigate into the local project directory.
details: Run cd your-project-name.
primary_path.output: Terminal working directory set to the project.
primary_path.success_signal: Project files are in the current directory.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A wrong name or destination produces a missing-directory error.

primary_path.action: Run hs project dev.
details: The development command checks dependencies and installs them as needed.
primary_path.input: Installed app, configured card, and local project.
primary_path.output: Account-selection prompt and local development startup.
primary_path.success_signal: The CLI prompts for the testing account.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Dependency installation, authentication, port, or project configuration can block startup.

primary_path.action: Select the account for the testing session.
details: Choose an already connected account for this primary path.
The CLI can instead create a new developer test or sandbox account through additional prompts.
primary_path.input: Connected HubSpot account containing the installed app and a contact.
primary_path.success_signal: Local development starts against the chosen account.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Selecting another account may require installing the app and ensuring a contact exists there.

primary_path.action: Install any missing dependencies and start the local development session.
details: The CLI performs dependency checking automatically.
Once started, HubSpot opens the Local Dev Panel in a browser.
primary_path.output: Running development server and browser Local Dev Panel.
primary_path.success_signal: The Local Dev Panel shows project build information and Get Started App Card.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Dependency downloads and server startup add undocumented waits; failures remain in terminal output.

primary_path.action: Click Preview next to Get Started App Card.
primary_path.success_signal: The contacts list opens.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If no Preview action appears, verify app installation, card setup, selected account, and server state.

primary_path.action: Click the name of a contact.
details: The guide assumes at least one contact is available.
primary_path.input: Contacts index with a contact record.
primary_path.success_signal: The selected contact opens.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An empty account requires creating or importing a contact, which the Quickstart does not document.

primary_path.action: In the Overview tab's middle column, scroll to and verify the app card.
details: The Developing locally badge is visible only to the developer while the local development server is running.
primary_path.input: Contact record and active local development session.
primary_path.success_signal: Get Started App Card displays with the Developing locally badge.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing card points to placement, account, install, server, or contact-view problems that the Quickstart does not diagnose in detail.

branches.condition: The user lacks Developer tools access.
branches.path: Have a super admin or partner admin grant the relevant permission or use an authorized user before generating the Personal Access Key.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds administrator coordination before CLI authentication and project upload.

branches.condition: The authenticated account is not set as default.
branches.path: Supply --account on applicable commands or select the account at prompts; hs account use can set it later.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds repeated account selection and risk of targeting the wrong account.

branches.condition: The developer chooses a new developer test account.
branches.path: Select the new-test-account option, enter its requested name or configuration, let HubSpot create it, install the app there through the prompts, ensure a contact exists, then start the testing session.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds account creation, app installation, and test-data setup while isolating real HubSpot data.

branches.condition: The developer chooses an existing developer test or sandbox account.
branches.path: Select it, complete any prompted app install, and use its contact data for the preview.
branches.effect_on_first_success: May require account access, entitlement, and installation but avoids the standard account's production data.

branches.condition: The developer continues the Quickstart development loop.
branches.path: Edit the app card's React/TypeScript file and save it to observe automatic local update; for JSON configuration changes, run hs project upload or click Upload and build.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Demonstrates hot reload and rebuild behavior after the first card is already visible.

friction_gates.description: A standard HubSpot account is required before the Quickstart begins.
Node.js 20+ and npm are needed for the HubSpot CLI.
The HubSpot CLI must be installed, potentially requiring global npm write permission.
The Personal Access Key's scopes are bounded by the HubSpot user's permissions; Developer tools access is needed for project upload.
A per-user, per-account Personal Access Key authenticates the CLI and must be protected.
HubSpot must build and deploy the project before installation.
The installation page displays an expected unverified-app warning and requires explicit confirmation.
The developer must choose a connected, test, or sandbox account for the local session.
Missing dependencies may be installed and the local development server must start.
The final preview requires a contact record in the selected account.

sources.title: Quickstart
sections_used: Install the app and set up the card

sources.title: Install the HubSpot CLI
sections_used: Install the latest version of the HubSpot CLI

sources.title: HubSpot Account Types
evidence_supported: Creation and limits of developer test accounts

sources.title: Authenticate the CLI with personal access keys

uncertainties.question: What exact forms and verification steps create a new standard HubSpot account?
uncertainties.impact: New developers may encounter uncounted pages before CLI setup.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The Quickstart links to account creation but does not enumerate the hosted signup flow.

uncertainties.question: What exact permission or admin flow grants Developer tools access in a given account?
uncertainties.impact: A user's Personal Access Key may authenticate but still fail to upload the project.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The key documentation explains permission inheritance but does not include the administrator grant workflow.

uncertainties.question: What happens if the selected account has no contacts?
uncertainties.impact: The documented final Preview path cannot open a contact to show the card.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The Quickstart says to click a contact but does not create seed data or state that the account is pre-populated.

uncertainties.question: How long do the first build, deploy, dependency install, and local server startup take?
uncertainties.impact: These waits contribute to real time to first visible card.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: HubSpot documents the waits but makes no duration claim.

excluded_after_success: Editing the React/TypeScript example and observing hot reload.
Uploading JSON configuration changes and rebuilding.
Adding data fetching, settings, webhooks, workflows, or other app features.
OAuth distribution, allowlists, marketplace listing, and app verification.
Production monitoring, API usage, and release management.
